preamble: The development of a final glottal stop [ʔ] (represented here as empty to glottal_stop) is a precursor to Tonogenesis. This Laryngeal Coda originally triggered a rising Pitch Contour on the preceding vowel, which would eventually become phonemicized as a rising tone (Shàng sheng or hỏi/ngã) upon the subsequent elision of the glottal stop.

examples.note: Historical roots in Mon-Khmer developed final glottalization (Laryngeal Coda), which then led to the rising tone in Vietnamese.
The 'Rising' category (Shàng sheng) in Middle Chinese is reconstructed as having acquired a final [ʔ] which influenced the Pitch Contour.
The interaction of laryngeal features and vowel duration in Tai-Kadai languages provided the phonetic basis for the complex tonal systems observed today.

sources: Haudricourt, A. G. (1954). De l'origine des tons en viêtnamien.
Matisoff, J. A. (1973). Tonogenesis in Southeast Asia.
